Summary
Historian SE - Race Condition Leading to Authentication Bypass
The security of our products is important to us as your industrial automation supplier. This security issue was found internally during routine testing and is being reported based on our commitment to customer transparency and improvement of all business environments.
Product Description
FactoryTalk® Historian Site Edition (SE) is an on-premises industrial data platform that collects, stores, analyzes, and contextualizes time-series process data across the plant, enabling real-time insights for performance optimization and enterprise-wide visibility.
Affected Products and Solution
Affected Product
CVE
Affected Software Version
Corrected in Software Version
Affected Catalog Numbers
Historian SE
CVE-2025-13036
11.00
12.00
None
Security Issue Details for CVE-2025-13036
Category
Details
CVE ID
CVE-2025-13036
Impact
A security issue exists within Historian SE, allowing an unauthenticated attacker to obtain a valid authentication token by exploiting a race condition in the login flow. The vulnerability stems from improper synchronization of shared resources during concurrent authentication requests. By continually sending login requests with invalid credentials while a legitimate user attempts to authenticate, an attacker can retrieve a valid session token.
CVSS 3.1 Base Score
9.4/10
CVSS 4.0 Base Score
9.2/10
CWE
CWE-362: Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization ('Race Condition')
Known Exploited Vulnerability
No
Mitigations and Workarounds
Customers using the affected software, who are not able to upgrade to one of the corrected versions, should use our
security best practices
. If customers can not upgrade, please consider applying the following
patch.
